preconditions: KOZO source-level heartbeat contract is present
Odin and Rust toolchains are installed
assumptions: This phase proves that the current Rust-to-assembly-to-Odin function-call boundary returns observable payload mutations back to Rust without introducing privilege transitions or a syscall instruction
K_INVALID is the current generated error-equivalent status constant

steps.title: Implement the ordered Odin syscall arbiter path and the Rust consumer contract
Add execution proof validation and detailed verify artifact reporting
Correct verification reproducibility and explicit stub-mode classification
Replace the Rust stub with the assembly bridge runtime trap path
Lock the canonical runtime trap wording and ignore generated Python caches
Remove tracked Python cache artifacts from version control
Prove that Rust observes returned payload mutations after the bridge call
Harden bridge alignment against dead or unordered assembly snippets
Harden runtime trap path proof against dead Rust bridge snippets
Harden execution proof around the live Odin heartbeat branch
Enforce validator test coverage governance
Enforce validator coverage depth governance
Introduce ABI manifest v0 and wire protocol and layout proofs
Introduce syscall boundary contract v0 for the heartbeat trap path
Validate live source conformance to syscall boundary contract v0
Introduce syscall table contract v0 for heartbeat dispatch
Validate live dispatcher conformance to syscall table contract v0
Govern K_SYSCALL_NOP as a no-payload syscall table entry
Exercise K_SYSCALL_NOP from Rust with a null payload bridge call
Introduce syscall class contract v0 for currently proven syscall shapes
Govern K_SYSCALL_STATUS as a no-payload status syscall
Add syscall catalog v0 for currently governed syscalls
Add generated syscall surface report
Add generated ABI surface report
Add governance document authority split
Pin Rust toolchain version
Add generated ABI binding self-identification headers
Select the pinned Rust toolchain through rustup during verification
Add generated governance index report
Document ABI validator shim and prune unused ABI generator helpers
Add v1.0.0 phase map, roadmap, and release evidence policy
Add release checklist and required checks policy
Add runtime smoke evidence baseline
Package runtime smoke evidence metadata
Add documentation clarity governance standard

expected_outputs: The verification script is reproducible on a clean tree and emits the current verify artifact
The kernel builds as a binary and as an object artifact without relying on broken default serial inline assembly
The Rust service crosses the assembly bridge through extern syscall_entry instead of a local stub
The harness records protocol_contract_alignment, runtime_trap_path, and execution_proof results that match the current runtime-trap implementation
README.md and ADR-0016 share the canonical wording for the function-call trap path and the workspace ignores generated __pycache__ content
Tracked Python cache artifacts are removed from version control so ignored cache files no longer dirty the workspace
Rust explicitly validates returned payload mutations after syscall_entry and the verify artifact records return_path_proof
Focused return_path_proof tests cover status_bits negative paths and unrelated status_bits text
bridge_alignment validates the ordered live syscall_entry block and rejects dead or unordered bridge snippets
runtime_trap_path validates the live Rust heartbeat request path with focused positive and negative tests
execution_proof validates the live Odin heartbeat branch and serial observations with focused positive and negative tests
The regenerated verification artifact records the hardened bridge, runtime, return-path, and execution proofs
validator_coverage prevents registered validators from entering the harness without focused behavioral negative-path tests
validator_coverage requires declared failure-mode markers to map to behavioral negative tests
ABI manifest v0 provides the Python-side source of truth for proven syscall constants, status constants, heartbeat payload layout, and heartbeat sentinels
Syscall boundary contract v0 describes and validates only the currently proven x86_64 heartbeat trap boundary
syscall_boundary_conformance proves live assembly, Rust, and Odin sources conform to syscall boundary contract v0
Syscall table contract v0 describes and validates the currently proven heartbeat/debug dispatcher branch and unknown-syscall default behavior
syscall_table_conformance proves the live Odin dispatcher implements syscall table contract v0 without uncontracted payload-mutating branches
K_SYSCALL_NOP is governed as a first-class no-payload syscall table entry that returns K_OK and never mutates or inspects heartbeat payload state
The Rust core service exercises K_SYSCALL_NOP through syscall_entry with a null payload pointer and validates the K_OK return status
Syscall class contract v0 classifies only currently proven syscall shapes and validates syscall table class assignments
K_SYSCALL_STATUS is governed as a no-payload status syscall with a null payload argument, K_OK return status, no payload mutation, live Odin dispatch, and a Rust bridge probe
Syscall catalog v0 summarizes nop, status, and debug_heartbeat without owning ABI constants, table structure, class semantics, or runtime behavior
docs/generated/syscall_surface.md provides a deterministic non-authoritative Markdown report generated from the syscall catalog and existing contracts
docs/generated/abi_surface.md provides a deterministic non-authoritative Markdown report generated from the ABI manifest

Governance documents split authority across precedence, invariants, architecture, contracts, coding style, validation, generated artifacts, compatibility, security model, and ADR policy without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, or compatibility behavior
Rust verification uses pinned cargo and rustc version 1.96.0
Release planning documents define phase sequencing, roadmap direction, and release evidence requirements without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, or compatibility behavior
Release governance baseline execution defines release checklist and required checks policy without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, or compatibility behavior
Runtime smoke evidence baseline records a bounded runtime-adjacent object and symbol evidence artifact without changing ABI, syscall, compatibility, or production-readiness claims
Runtime smoke evidence packaging records deterministic metadata and release bundle guidance without adding QEMU boot, hardware trap, compatibility, userspace, or production-readiness claims

Documentation standard v1 governs documentation clarity, audience separation, claim discipline, terminology consistency, onboarding structure, and documentation review without changing runtime, ABI, syscall, validator, compatibility, or production-readiness claims

invariants: The default kernel build path must succeed without broken serial inline assembly
The Rust heartbeat request must cross the assembly bridge through extern syscall_entry rather than a local stub
The verify artifact is only authoritative when the current tree reproduces it via scripts/verify.sh
expected_behavior: verify.sh succeeds on a clean tree and prints the generated artifact JSON before VERIFY: PASS
odin build kernel succeeds with the default source tree
odin build kernel -build-mode:obj -out:artifacts/kernel.o succeeds and produces a host-build object artifact
verify.sh emits runtime_trap_path: pass when Rust declares and calls extern syscall_entry
Reintroducing the local Rust stub or removing the extern bridge call causes runtime_trap_path to fail
Changing the expected Rust sequence to the wrong value causes return_path_proof to fail
Removing the Rust timestamp check causes return_path_proof to fail
Removing the Rust status_bits check causes return_path_proof to fail
Removing the Odin status_bits success write causes return_path_proof to fail
Moving required bridge snippets outside the live syscall_entry block causes bridge_alignment to fail
Reordering required live syscall_entry anchors causes bridge_alignment to fail
Moving required Rust bridge snippets outside the live helper causes runtime_trap_path to fail
Changing heartbeat request sentinels or ordering causes runtime_trap_path to fail
Moving required Odin mutations outside the live heartbeat branch causes execution_proof to fail
Removing nil guards, status_bits mutation, or serial observations causes execution_proof to fail
A registered validator without a focused test file causes validator_coverage to fail
A placeholder negative-looking test without validator invocation or failure assertion causes validator_coverage to fail
Missing KOZO_NEGATIVE_COVERAGE metadata causes validator_coverage to fail
Missing required markers, unknown markers, missing mapped tests, and non-behavioral mapped tests cause validator_coverage to fail
Removing ABI surface report constants, binding paths, layout fields, sentinels, or generated content causes abi_surface_report to fail
Governance document updates remain bounded to documentation authority, changelog state, and task scope
Verification fails if cargo or rustc do not report pinned version 1.96.0

Release planning documents preserve non-goals for Linux compatibility, POSIX completeness, general userspace execution, process model behavior, VFS behavior, scheduler behavior, ELF loading, file descriptor behavior, and production readiness

Release checklist and required checks policy preserve non-goals for Linux compatibility, POSIX completeness, general userspace execution, process model behavior, VFS behavior, scheduler behavior, ELF loading, file descriptor behavior, and production readiness

Runtime smoke evidence preserves non-goals for Linux compatibility, POSIX completeness, general userspace execution, process model behavior, VFS behavior, scheduler behavior, ELF loading, file descriptor behavior, QEMU boot execution, hardware trap execution, and production readiness

Runtime smoke metadata preserves explicit non-goals for QEMU boot, hardware trap execution, Linux compatibility, userspace execution, process model, VFS behavior, scheduler maturity, ELF loading, file descriptor behavior, and production readiness

Documentation standard changes remain bounded to documentation construction governance, governance precedence, changelog state, task scope, generated governance index refresh, and verification artifact refresh
actual_behavior: verify.sh handles an empty changed-files set without aborting and prints the generated verify artifact.
The kernel builds successfully as both the default binary target and the object artifact target.
core_service declares and calls extern syscall_entry instead of a local heartbeat stub.
The verify artifact reports protocol_contract_alignment and runtime_trap_path for the current runtime-trap implementation.
README.md and ADR-0016 use the same canonical statement for the function-call trap path, and __pycache__/ is ignored.
Tracked Python cache artifacts are removed from version control so the remaining __pycache__ files are ignored.
core_service validates the returned status, sequence, timestamp, and status_bits after syscall_entry and the verify artifact reports return_path_proof.
Changing the expected Rust sequence to 0xBADFEED causes return_path_proof to fail.
Removing the Rust timestamp check causes return_path_proof to fail.
Removing the Rust status_bits check causes return_path_proof to fail with a named missing-anchor diagnostic.
Removing the Odin status_bits success write causes return_path_proof to fail with a named missing-anchor diagnostic.
A stray status_bits mention does not satisfy the required Rust return-path status_bits check.
bridge_alignment rejects dead snippets outside syscall_entry with a named diagnostic.
bridge_alignment rejects out-of-order live syscall_entry anchors with a named diagnostic.
bridge_alignment rejects missing dispatcher handoff and missing Odin dispatcher signatures.
runtime_trap_path rejects dead extern bridge calls outside the live helper with a named diagnostic.
runtime_trap_path rejects missing request anchors, wrong request sentinels, and out-of-order live path construction.
execution_proof rejects dead Odin mutation snippets outside the live heartbeat branch with a named diagnostic.
execution_proof rejects missing nil guards, missing status_bits mutation, out-of-order mutations, and missing serial observation strings.
scripts/verify.sh regenerated latest_verify.json after the hardened validator commits.
validator_coverage reports pass only when every registered validator has focused tests with behavioral negative-path coverage.
A placeholder test containing only a validator token and negative-looking name fails validator_coverage with a named missing-invocation diagnostic.
validator_coverage reports pass only when each declared negative marker maps to an existing behavioral negative test.
Missing marker metadata, missing required markers, unknown markers, missing mapped test functions, and mapped placeholder tests fail with named diagnostics.
K_SYSCALL_NOP is represented in syscall table contract v0 as a no-payload entry that requires abi.K_SYSCALL_NOP, returns K_OK, and rejects payload mutation or heartbeat payload layout usage.
The Rust NOP probe calls syscall_entry with abi::K_SYSCALL_NOP and core::ptr::null_mut(), validates abi::K_OK, and is exercised before the heartbeat request.

syscall_class_contract validates no_payload_status and payload_mutating_status semantics, requires table entries to declare classes, rejects kind/class drift, forbids request/response metadata on no-payload entries, and rejects missing payload-mutating metadata.

K_SYSCALL_STATUS is represented in the ABI manifest, syscall table contract, syscall class contract, live Odin dispatcher, and Rust core service probe as a no-payload status syscall that returns K_OK and does not inspect or mutate payload state.
syscall_catalog validates the cataloged syscall summaries against the ABI manifest, syscall table contract, syscall class contract, registered proof validators, and live Rust runtime probe presence.

syscall_surface_report validates that docs/generated/syscall_surface.md matches the deterministic renderer output and fails on stale content, manual edits, missing syscall sections, missing class summaries, and missing source references.

abi_surface_report validates that docs/generated/abi_surface.md matches the deterministic renderer output and fails on stale content, manual edits, missing constants, missing binding paths, missing layout details, missing sentinels, and manifest-driven drift.

Governance documents now define document precedence, non-negotiable invariants, code construction rules, contract authority, validation policy, generated artifact policy, compatibility boundaries, security boundary rules, and ADR requirements while keeping architecture diagrams descriptive.
rust-toolchain.toml pins Rust 1.96.0 and scripts/verify.sh resolves and invokes cargo and rustc from the pinned toolchain directly.

governance_index_report validates that docs/generated/governance_index.md matches the deterministic renderer output for active contracts, schemas, validators, generated reports, latest verification artifact, current version, and non-goals.
harness/validators_impl/abi.py remains a documented compatibility shim for the registered abi validator while unused ABI generator helpers are pruned.

PHASEMAP.md, ROADMAP.md, and docs/RELEASE_EVIDENCE.md define release sequencing, direction, gates, evidence, and blocker categories without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, or compatibility claims.

docs/RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md and docs/REQUIRED_CHECKS.md define release approval and required-check policy while docs/RELEASE_EVIDENCE.md, PHASEMAP.md, and ROADMAP.md link to those policies without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, or compatibility claims.

scripts/runtime_smoke.sh generates artifacts/runtime/runtime_smoke.log from freestanding kernel and assembly object evidence, and runtime_smoke_evidence validates the artifact while explicitly documenting that it is not QEMU boot or hardware trap evidence.

scripts/runtime_smoke.sh generates deterministic artifacts/runtime/runtime_smoke.metadata.json, runtime_smoke_evidence validates metadata claims and non-goals, and release evidence docs define live and packaged runtime evidence paths.

docs/DOCUMENTATION_STANDARD.md defines documentation construction and review standards while docs/GOVERNANCE.md records its authority boundary below higher technical governance documents and above descriptive documents.

explanation_summary: Made scripts/verify.sh reproducible on clean trees, added fresh build/check evidence generation, and emitted the generated artifact JSON directly.
Replaced the broken default serial inline assembly path with a build-safe stub so kernel builds and object generation succeed consistently.
Replaced the Rust heartbeat stub with an extern syscall_entry bridge call, added runtime_trap_path validation, and aligned the repository status with the exercised assembly bridge boundary.
Locked the canonical runtime-trap wording into README.md and ADR-0016 and ignored generated __pycache__/ directories to keep the workspace clean.
Removed the tracked Python cache artifacts from version control so ignored cache files no longer pollute repository state.
Added return_path_proof so the Rust caller must explicitly validate the returned payload mutations after the bridge call, with negative proofs for wrong sequence and missing timestamp checks.
Hardened return_path_proof so status_bits checks and writes are required anchors with focused negative unit coverage.
Hardened bridge_alignment so ordered live syscall_entry anchors are required and covered by focused negative tests.
Hardened runtime_trap_path so the live Rust heartbeat request path and bridge helper are required and covered by focused negative tests.
Hardened execution_proof so the live Odin heartbeat branch and stable serial observations are required and covered by focused negative tests.
Regenerated latest_verify.json so the current artifact records the hardened proof details.
Added validator_coverage so registered validators must have focused test files with AST-verified behavioral negative-path coverage.
Extended validator_coverage so registered validators must declare marker-level coverage for their known false-pass risks.
Promoted K_SYSCALL_NOP into a governed no-payload syscall table entry without adding payload layout, userspace execution, Linux compatibility, or broader runtime behavior.
Added a narrow Rust NOP probe through the existing syscall_entry bridge using a null payload argument and no payload mutation.
Added syscall class contract v0 so currently proven syscall table entries declare semantic classes without adding new syscalls or runtime behavior.

Added K_SYSCALL_STATUS as a governed no-payload status syscall without adding Linux compatibility, userspace generalization, process model behavior, VFS behavior, scheduler behavior, ELF loading, file descriptor behavior, or runtime subsystem behavior.
Added syscall catalog v0 as a governed summary of existing syscall contracts without adding new syscalls or runtime behavior.
Added generated syscall surface report as non-authoritative Markdown derived from existing contracts without adding new syscalls or runtime behavior.
Added generated ABI surface report as non-authoritative Markdown derived from the ABI manifest without changing ABI constants, ABI layouts, generated bindings, syscalls, or runtime behavior.
Added governance document authority split without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall contracts, generated bindings, validator logic, or compatibility behavior.
Pinned Rust verification to cargo and rustc 1.96.0 without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall contracts, generated bindings, or validator logic.
Updated verification and CI to select the Rust toolchain declared by rust-toolchain.toml and invoke its cargo and rustc binaries directly instead of relying on ambient cargo or host stable dispatch.

Added generated governance index as a non-authoritative report over active governance surfaces without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall contracts, generated bindings, or compatibility behavior.
Documented the ABI validator shim and pruned unused ABI generator helpers without changing generated bindings, ABI contracts, runtime behavior, or validator behavior.
Added release planning documents for KOZO v1.0.0 without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, or compatibility claims.
Added release checklist and required checks policy for v0.1.0 release governance baseline execution without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, or compatibility claims.

Added a bounded runtime smoke evidence baseline for v0.2.0 using freestanding object and symbol evidence without adding runtime features, ABI changes, syscall changes, compatibility claims, or production-readiness claims.

Added runtime evidence packaging metadata and release bundle guidance for v0.2.1 without adding runtime features, ABI changes, syscall changes, QEMU boot claims, compatibility claims, or production-readiness claims.

Added documentation clarity governance standard without changing runtime behavior, ABI contracts, syscall behavior, generated bindings, validator logic, compatibility claims, or production-readiness claims.
